LAW AND MOTION 302, SPECIALLY APPEARING DEFENDANTS HINES 101 SECOND STREET LP AND HINES INTEREST LIMITED PARTNERSHIP'S MOTION TO DISMISS AND MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS IS GRANTED. EXCEPT OF THE LESS THAN ONE MONTH PERIOD (MARCH 23, 2011 THROUGH APRIL 20, 2011) FROM THE ENTRY OF JUDGMENT TO THE SETTING ASIDE OF THAT JUDGMENT, NONE OF THE PROFFERED REASONS FOR TOLLING THE MANDATORY THREE YEAR PERIOD MADE IT IMPOSSIBLE, IMPRACTICABLE OR FUTILE FOR PLAINTIFF TO SERVE THE MOVING PARTIES WITHIN THREE YEARS OF PLAINTIFF FILING HER COMPLAINT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

Off Calendar Apr-06-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. LORENA MATEI OF LEWIS, BRISBOIS, BISGAARD & SMITH, LLP, TEL. NO. 438-6639 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS AND MOVING PARTIES;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. SPECIALLY APPEARING DEFENDANTS HINES 101 SECOND STREET LP AND HINES INTEREST LIMITED PARTNERSHIP'S Motion To Dismiss And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons is Granted. Except of the less than one month period (March 23, 2011 through April 20, 2011) from the entry of judgment to the setting aside of that judgment, none of the proffered reasons for tolling the mandatory three year period made it impossible, impracticable or futile for plaintiff to serve the moving parties within three years of plaintiff filing her complaint. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported. = (302/HEK)

APPEARANCES - ATTY. SALLY MORIN OF MILLER & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 981-8100 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS Denied. There is a triable issue whether the City had constructive notice of the dangerous condition a sufficient time prior to the injury to have taken measures to protect against it.
